channel.title: AI on xuanjin
channel.description: Recent content in AI on xuanjin

item.title: AI & Enterprise - Adoption
item.description: If you haven’t read through a couple of agent-related posts, you probably should first read this post. For enterprises, what we can learn from OpenClaw or OpenClaw-like tools. First off, I think we need to take a look at the AI tools out there: Foundation Model Platforms Microsoft Copilot + Azure AI Studio — deeply integrated into Microsoft 365, Dynamics, Azure Google Cloud Vertex AI + Gemini — tight Google Workspace integration, strong on multimodal AWS Bedrock — Amazon’s managed LLM platform, Claude/Anthropic available here too IBM watsonx — strong in regulated industries (finance, healthcare, government) Business/Productivity AI

item.title: OpenClaw, How I have been using it, What Enterprise Can Do With It Potentially
item.description: I have been playing with OpenClaw for a while now, I think I started around middle of March maybe. It has been about a month now. Everything I have learned about AI has been from using OpenClaw. A little bit about me — the way I learn things, all kinds of things, is hands-on. I need to actually work on something to have that “thing” make sense to me. I know my assistant Gideon has already written something for me on 4/2, which you can find here.
